ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Ma gba tiŋŋa puusi Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni, so n pa ma yiya, a ni seema n daga, ma nye m yidaana Ibrahim mabii yaŋŋu ti o biya.
translated: I was sitting with my father, Ibrahim Naawunni, when he told me that I should go, and I got up, and my father Ibrahim gave me his horse to ride.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: Tiŋgbaŋaa m ni daa n naa n ti Ibrahim ni Yisaaka la, m naag u ti i, ka m ni naag u ti e yaŋsi”.
translated: When I was with Ibrahim and Isaac, I was their friend, and I will be your friend, and I will be a friend to your descendants.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Niŋi n na, guruŋu maa naa o yidaana nyogimasi piiya, ka naa piinisuma baligu zaa paasi n gaari Mesopoteemiya paaligu, Naho yiri.
translated: The great king, the mighty king of the land between the rivers, the king of the four quarters of the world, the king of Mesopotamia, the land of Nahor.

ref: Then he went back to his brothers and said, “The boy is gone! What am I going to do?”
text: Ka we o sunzoba la saani, a wa ti boosi ba, “Biyaa lee n ka bulugu maa puwa? Wula n na ma ya n niŋi?”
translated: I went to the beautiful forest, and I met the hunters, and they asked me, "Where are you going with your load? What do you have in it?"

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni malaaka goo yi saazu tasi n booli u yeli, “Ibrahim, Ibrahim.” O sagi, “Mani n ŋa.”
translated: The angel of God called out to him from heaven and said, "Abraham, Abraham." He replied, "Here I am."
